Holy Symbol.description: A representation of a god or pantheon. It might be an amulet depicting a symbol representing a deity, the same symbol carefully engraved or inlaid as an emblem on a shield, or a tiny box holding a fragment of a sacred relic. A cleric or paladin can use a holy symbol as a spellcasting focus. Must be held in hand, worn visibly, or borne on a shield.

Holy Water.description: As an action, splash contents onto a creature within 5 feet or throw up to 20 feet, shattering on impact. Make a ranged attack treating it as an improvised weapon. Fiends or undead take 2d6 radiant damage. Can be created by a cleric/paladin via a 1-hour ritual expending a 1st-level spell slot.

Shield.description: Made from wood or metal and is carried in one hand. Wielding a shield increases your Armor Class by 2. You can benefit from only one shield at a time.

Padded Armor.description: Padded armor consists of quilted layers of cloth and batting.

Leather Armor.description: The breastplate and shoulder protectors of this armor are made of leather that has been stiffened by being boiled in oil. The rest of the armor is made of softer and more flexible materials.

Studded Leather.description: Made from tough but flexible leather, studded leather is reinforced with close-set rivets or spikes.

Hide Armor.description: This crude armor consists of thick furs and pelts.

Chain Shirt.description: Made of interlocking metal rings, worn between layers of clothing or leather. Offers modest protection and muffles the sound of rings rubbing together.

Scale Mail.description: Consists of a coat and leggings of leather covered with overlapping pieces of metal, much like fish scales. Includes gauntlets.

Breastplate.description: A fitted metal chest piece worn with supple leather. Provides good protection for vital organs while leaving the wearer relatively unencumbered.

Half Plate.description: Shaped metal plates covering most of the body. Does not include leg protection beyond simple greaves attached with leather straps.

Chain Mail.description: Made of interlocking metal rings, includes a layer of quilted fabric underneath to prevent chafing. Includes gauntlets.

Priest's Pack.description: Includes a backpack, a blanket, 10 candles, a tinderbox, an alms box, 2 blocks of incense, a censer, vestments, 2 days of rations, and a waterskin.

Explorer's Pack.description: Includes a backpack, a bedroll, a mess kit, a tinderbox, 10 torches, 10 days of rations, a waterskin, and 50 feet of hempen rope.

Mace.properties: Sap (If you hit a creature, it has Disadvantage on its next attack roll before the start of your next turn.)

Warhammer.properties: Versatile (1d10), Push (If you hit, you can push the creature up to 10 feet away if it is Large or smaller.)

Light Crossbow.properties: Ammunition (Range 80/320), Loading, Two-Handed, Slow (If you hit and deal damage, reduce target Speed by 10 feet until the start of your next turn.)

Crossbow Bolts.description: Used with a weapon that has the ammunition property. Recover half your expended ammunition by taking a minute to search the battlefield after battle.

Fate Dealer's Deck.attunement: Requires attunement by a Cleric: Divine Seer Domain
Fate Dealer's Deck.description: Used as a spellcasting focus. Grants a bonus to spell attack rolls and spell save DC based on rarity (Rare: +1, Very Rare: +2, Legendary: +3). When drawing a card from 'Draw of Fate', you can expend a Hit Die and add the deck's bonus to the roll to deal radiant damage or heal a creature within 30 ft.
